title: Quiz: Noise in NISQ Devices
description: Test your understanding of noise sources and effects in NISQ devices

questions.question: What physical process does T₁ relaxation describe?
questions.explanation: T₁ relaxation describes energy decay where the qubit spontaneously transitions from the excited state |1⟩ to the ground state |0⟩.

questions.question: Which noise channel primarily affects off-diagonal elements of the density matrix?
questions.explanation: Phase damping (T₂ dephasing) primarily affects coherence terms (off-diagonal elements) while preserving populations (diagonal elements).

questions.question: How is a quantum channel mathematically represented?

options.text: Sum of Kraus operators: ρ' = Σᵢ Kᵢ ρ Kᵢ†

questions.explanation: Quantum channels are represented using Kraus operators: ρ' = Σᵢ Kᵢ ρ Kᵢ†, where the operators satisfy Σᵢ Kᵢ†Kᵢ = I.

questions.question: What does the depolarizing channel do?

options.text: Rotates the state around the Z-axis
Mixes the state toward the maximally mixed state

questions.explanation: The depolarizing channel applies random Pauli errors, effectively mixing the state toward the maximally mixed state I/2.

questions.question: Deeper quantum circuits always accumulate more noise, leading to worse fidelity.
questions.explanation: True. Each gate introduces errors, so deeper circuits accumulate more noise and generally have lower fidelity with the ideal result.

questions.question: What does randomized benchmarking measure?
questions.explanation: Randomized benchmarking measures the average error per Clifford gate by fitting exponential decay of survival probability with sequence length.

questions.question: What is a noise-induced barren plateau?

options.text: Gradient decay due to circuit expressibility
Gradient decay due to accumulated noise with depth
Optimization getting stuck at a local minimum

questions.explanation: Noise-induced barren plateaus occur when accumulated noise causes gradient variance to decay exponentially with circuit depth, independent of ansatz design.

questions.question: Which are common noise sources in NISQ devices? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: T₁ relaxation, gate errors, and measurement errors are all common noise sources. While cosmic rays can cause errors, they're not a primary noise source in typical operation.
